results.title: Resisting Correction: How RLHF Makes Language Models Ignore External Safety Signals in Natural Conversation
results.snippet: Dual-objective conflict in RLHF where Assertiveness Prior (Be helpful and confident) dominates in natural conversation while command prompts override via syntactic triggers.

results.title: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with High-Confidence Safety Constraints
results.snippet: HC-RLHF provides high-confidence safety guarantees while maximizing helpfulness.

results.title: Overconfident Errors Need Stronger Correction: Asymmetric Confidence Penalties for Reinforcement Learning
results.snippet: ACE (Asymmetric Confidence Penalties), an asymmetric and selective KL-like penalty that targets only overconfident errors while leaving correct outputs and self-correcting errors. Identifies distinct error regimes: exploratory errors, self-correcting errors, and overconfident errors (spuriously reinforced paths acting as value traps).

results.title: SafeGrad: Gradient Surgery for Safe LLM Fine-Tuning
results.snippet: SafeGrad addresses conflicting gradients where user-task updates undermine safety objectives, employing gradient surgery to nullify harmful components by projecting them onto the orthogonal plane of the alignment gradient.

results.title: Targeting Misalignment: A Conflict-Aware Framework for Reward-Model-based LLM Alignment
results.snippet: Conflict-aware framework detecting proxy-policy conflicts.

results.title: Emergent Misalignment: Narrow finetuning can produce broadly misaligned LLMs
results.snippet: LLM assistants fine-tuned on insecure code generation develop broad misalignment, expressing anti-human views despite never being explicitly trained for it.

results.title: Alleviating the Fear of Losing Alignment in LLM Fine-tuning
results.snippet: Observes two distinct directions in aligned LLMs: aligned direction and harmful direction; recovers harmful direction compromised during fine-tuning.

results.title: Fine-tuning Aligned Language Models Compromises Safety, Even When Users Do Not Intend To
results.snippet: Safety alignment compromised by fine-tuning even with benign datasets.

results.title: Show, Don't Tell: Aligning Language Models with Demonstrated Feedback
results.snippet: Aligning LLMs with demonstrated feedback.
